title: D2 error codes spec (canonical generalized schema)
description: Canonical generalized schema for all *-error-codes spec files — the source-of-truth catalogs the unified error-codes generator reads to emit <Domain>ErrorCodes.g.cs + <Domain>Failures.g.cs (.NET) and <domain>-error-codes.g.ts + <domain>-failures.g.ts (TypeScript) for each domain. The generic cross-cutting spec (contracts/error-codes/error-codes.spec.json) and every per-domain spec (contracts/<domain>-error-codes/<domain>-error-codes.spec.json) conform to this shape. Per-domain schema.json files either copy this definition verbatim or $ref it directly. The generator derives the emitted class names (<Domain>ErrorCodes / <Domain>Failures) and the enforced domain-prefix constraint from the spec filename; per-domain codes MUST be prefixed with the domain token in SCREAMING_SNAKE (e.g. AUTH_*, GEO_*, KEYCUSTODIAN_*); the generic spec owns the sole reserved unprefixed namespace (NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, etc.); the merged-registry build hard-fails on any cross-catalog code collision. Example spec entry: { "code": "GEO_SUBDIVISION_NOT_FOUND", "httpStatus": 404, "category": "not_found", "userMessageKey": "TK.Geo.Errors.SUBDIVISION_NOT_FOUND", "factoryName": "SubdivisionNotFound", "factoryShape": "standard", "doc": "The requested ISO 3166-2 subdivision code does not exist." }

code.description: Wire-format error code (SCREAMING_SNAKE). Shape-only regex — the generator derives the domain prefix token from the catalog filename and enforces that every code in a per-domain spec starts with that token (e.g. AUTH_*, GEO_*). The generic catalog owns the unprefixed reserved namespace. Must be unique across the spec.

httpStatus.description: HTTP status mapping for transport. In a per-domain catalog it selects the base D2Result factory a <Domain>Failures factory delegates to (404 -> NotFound, 409 -> Conflict, 400 -> ValidationFailed, 401 -> Unauthorized, 403 -> Forbidden, 503 -> ServiceUnavailable, 500 -> UnhandledException, 413 -> PayloadTooLarge, 429 -> TooManyRequests). In the generic catalog each code defines its own named factory (see factoryName); multiple codes may share a status (e.g. VALIDATION_FAILED and CANCELED are both 400 but are distinct factories), so the emitted factory is identified by factoryName + factoryShape, not status alone. Expanding the supported set requires extending this enum and the generator's status validation.

category.description: Closed semantic/telemetry classification. NOT the factory selector — httpStatus drives factory selection. Added to the wire (gRPC trailers + JSON envelope) so consumers can do generic class-based handling (e.g. retry any infrastructure_unavailable) without importing the producer's catalog. partial_success classifies the read-partial (206 SOME_FOUND) + write-partial (207 PARTIAL_SUCCESS) codes, which are not failures.

userMessageKey.description: Translation key reference rendered as the messages argument on the emitted D2Result factory (e.g. TK.Auth.Errors.UNAUTHORIZED, TK.Common.Errors.NOT_FOUND). Written as the C# symbol-path string — the .NET emitter uses it verbatim in the factory body; the TS emitter passes it to tk(...). The generator enforces existence by inverse-transforming the symbol path to the snake_case key (TK.Auth.Errors.UNAUTHORIZED -> auth_errors_UNAUTHORIZED) and asserting membership in contracts/messages/en-US.json. Coarse on purpose for auth-class codes — one key per status bucket so the user-facing surface does not leak which validation step failed.

factoryName.description: PascalCase symbol for the emitted factory method on <Domain>Failures (e.g. BearerMissing, JwksUnavailable, SubdivisionNotFound). The .NET emitter uses it as-is; the TS emitter lowercases the first character to camelCase (bearerMissing). Must be unique across the spec.

factoryShape.description: Closed enum that drives the generated factory's signature variant. standard: the one universal error-factory shape (messages?, inputErrors?, errorCode?, category?, traceId?) — ALL optional. Every error factory at any status can stamp a domain code + category and optionally carry inputErrors; the errorCode / category overrides are how a delegating domain factory stamps a specific code + category on a base status. none: no factory generated for this code; only the constant + boolean are emitted — used by codes with no current factory (e.g. SOME_FOUND/PARTIAL_SUCCESS whose data-carrying 206/207 factories stay hand-rolled, IDEMPOTENCY_IN_FLIGHT/serialization codes).

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted constant + factory (.NET) and JSDoc text on the emitted TS constant + factory.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
